headline: Sugar Tax Revenue of RM600 Million Not Directed to Health Programs
context: Revenue from the sugar-sweetened beverage tax introduced in 2019 goes to general revenue rather than health promotion.

cards.big: RM600 million collected from sugar tax in 2025 but zero allocated to NCD prevention
cards.sub: Tax was introduced to reduce sugar consumption but revenue is not earmarked for health

cards.big: Sugar-sweetened beverage consumption dropped only 8% since 2019 tax introduction
cards.sub: Modest price increase of 12-15% is below the 20% threshold for meaningful behavior change

cards.big: Diabetes prevalence increased from 17.5% to 18.3% during the same period 2019 to 2025
cards.sub: Sugar tax alone without supporting interventions has not reversed the diabetes trend

cards.big: Mexico earmarks 100% of sugar tax for water fountain installation and health promotion
cards.sub: Hypothecated health taxes create direct link between revenue and health outcomes

cards.big: A sugar tax that funds general revenue instead of health programs is just another tax not a health intervention
Should all sugar tax revenue be earmarked exclusively for NCD prevention and health promotion
